addons.name: TR PDF Converter
addons.description: Native Trade Republic PDF parser — extracts cash, MMF, trading P&L, crypto. Direct one-click import to Donkeyfolio with quantity + unit price + fee. Multi-language statement support (DE/EN/PT/ES/IT/FR). Format-aware number parsing. Auto-recovery for broken rows. WKN extraction. Yahoo-based stock-split detection. Cash reconciliation panel with PDF SUMMARY block as ground truth and saldo-chain enforcement. v2.7.5 hotfix on top of v2.7.4 — empty-saldo rows (Card Transaction fragments etc.) were being treated as €0 saldo by the chain enforcer, creating €10k+ of symmetric phantom in/out drift. v2.7.5 properly skips fragment rows so the chain anchors against the last meaningful saldo. Ported from jcmpagel's open-source code.
addons.author: Donkeyfolio
addons.releaseNotes: v2.7.5: Hotfix on top of v2.7.4 — fixes the symmetric €10,303 phantom drift introduced by chain enforcement. Cause: parseEuroAmount('') returns 0 (not NaN), so when a fragment row from a multi-line Card Transaction layout (lines containing only '13' or 'Card' or 'Transaction' with no saldo column) was processed, the chain enforcer thought the running balance had dropped to €0, created a huge phantom OUT to bridge the gap, then created a matching huge phantom IN on the next valid row to bridge back up. Each pair contributed €Xk to BOTH IN and OUT totals symmetrically — totalling €10,303 in the user's case. v2.7.5 properly checks for 'meaningful saldo' (must contain at least one digit) and SKIPs fragment rows without advancing prevSaldo. The chain now anchors against the last real saldo seen, eliminating the phantom drift. v2.7.4: ROOT-CAUSE FIX — saldo-chain consistency enforcement. v2.7.3 surfaced an OUT drift of +€17,615 between row-by-row sum and PDF summary. Investigation against the user's 285-page TR statement revealed the cause: rows like Card Transactions have a multi-line layout in the PDF (day-line + 'Card'-line + month+merchant+amounts-line + 'Transaction'-line) that confuses the column-boundary parser, causing some In/Out values to be mis-classified or dropped. There are 111 Card Transactions in a typical TR yearly statement, plus many other layout quirks (large saldos overflowing column boundaries, multi-line descriptions). Rather than chase each individual layout edge case, v2.7.4 introduces enforceChainConsistency(): for each cash row, compare the parser's In/Out against (saldo[i] − saldo[i-1]). If they disagree by more than €0.01, rewrite In/Out to match the chain. By construction this guarantees sum(In) − sum(Out) = closing − opening, so the imported cash balance lines up with the PDF SUMMARY's ending balance EXACTLY — irrespective of any column-boundary parser bugs. Anchored to the PDF SUMMARY's openingBalance for the chain start. The chain-consistency corrections are rolled into the existing 'auto-corrected rows' counter, so the user sees how many rows had to be repaired. v2.7.3: PDF SUMMARY block as ground truth. Every TR statement starts with an 'ACCOUNT STATEMENT SUMMARY' table on page 1 that gives the authoritative period totals (opening balance, money in, money out, ending balance) — TR computes these directly so they bypass any row-by-row parsing fragility. Up to v2.7.2 we were summing cash rows ourselves to compute these totals; on a 285-page yearly statement that adds up to ~€16k of phantom outflows because column-boundary heuristics fail, page boundaries get confused, and saldo chains break in subtle ways. v2.7.3 introduces parseStatementSummary() in the vendored parser that locates the SUMMARY block (multi-language: EN/DE/PT/ES/IT/FR), extracts the 4 numbers, and threads them through to the reconciliation panel as the source-of-truth. The panel now displays the PDF summary numbers directly AND a 'parser drift' warning showing IN/OUT/closing diffs between row-by-row sum vs PDF summary — making it trivial to see when (and where) the row-level parser is dropping or double-counting data. Reconciliation gap is now computed against PDF summary's (ending - opening), not our row-sum guess. v2.7.2: Multi-language coverage. (1) Quantity extraction was EN-only. The regex `/quantity\s*:\s*([\d.,]+)/i` matched 'quantity: 1.234' but not the German 'Stück: 1,234' / 'Stk.: 1,234' / 'Anzahl: 1,234', the Portuguese 'quantidade: 1.234' / 'qtd: 1.234', the Spanish 'cantidad: 1,234', or the Italian 'quantità: 1,234' / 'pezzi: 1,234'. Result: every trade in a non-EN PDF lost its quantity, dropping the BUY/SELL activity (and on v2.7.0/v2.7.1 also dropping the cash leg via the skipKeys hole that was fixed in v2.7.1). v2.7.2 ships a unified label regex that covers all 6 languages and a parallel cleanup in the cleanStockName logic so localized statements don't leave 'Compra APPLE, quantidade: 1.234' as the displayed name. (2) Lookup bug: the cash→description index used `c.outgoing || c.incoming` which evaluates string-truthiness — a column containing only whitespace is truthy but parses to 0, so the lookup key was '...|0.00' and never matched the trade amount, silently losing the description even for EN PDFs in edge cases. Fixed to use parsed numeric values. (3) MMF section was DE-only. findInterestHeaders only recognised the German column titles (DATUM/ZAHLUNGSART/GELDMARKTFONDS/STÜCK/KURS PRO STÜCK/BETRAG); any user with a non-DE statement got 0 MMF rows even when the section was present in the PDF. v2.7.2 internationalizes the headers (DE/EN/PT/ES/IT/FR) with a synonym table and a normalized substring fallback (so 'FUNDO MERCADO MONETÁRIO' matches via 'FUNDO'). The interest-section start/end labels were also expanded to PT/ES/IT. v2.7.1: Cash-balance accuracy fixes. (1) CRITICAL FIX — skipKeys bug. The cash leg of trading rows was being skipped via date|ISIN keys, but those keys were built from ALL trades, including the ones we couldn't import (because their quantity wasn't extractable from the description). Net effect: ~98 trades on a typical TR yearly statement → ~€8,700 of cash flow silently evaporating (no BUY activity AND no WITHDRAWAL activity). Fix: skipKeys now only includes trades that will actually generate a BUY/SELL (qty > 0). The dropped trades' cash legs flow through as WITHDRAWAL/DEPOSIT, restoring the cash balance to TR's reported €10.5k. (2) Opening balance derivation — the reconciliation panel was computing opening = saldo[0] − firstSigned, which assumed the first row was the start of the statement. TR statements carry over balance from the prior period, so this gave opening=€0 even when the real opening was €17K+. v2.7.1 derives opening = closing − (totalIn − totalOut), which is exact by construction whenever the row sums add up. (3) Extended cash classification — added SAVEBACK (TR card cashback), REFUND (chargebacks/reimbursements), and SETTLEMENT (rebooking/rounding adjustments) row types. Direction-aware: outgoing settlements still flow to WITHDRAWAL. v2.7.0: Three verification & accuracy features. (1) WKN extraction — the German Wertpapierkennnummer is now pulled from each trade description and shown as a column in the Trading P&L table. WKNs also appear in import-failure samples (e.g. "US0080731088 (WKN A0MJX7): Quote currency required"), making it trivial to look the security up by its German ID when a mapping is wrong or missing. The lesson from v2.6.7's bad guesses: WKN unambiguously identifies the security where truncated PDF descriptions leave doubt. (2) Stock-split detection — opt-in 'Check for splits' button that queries Yahoo Finance's chart API for split events on every mapped Yahoo ticker, after each position's first trade date. Surfaces splits in a table (stock, ticker, WKN, split date, ratio, first trade) so the user knows exactly which SPLIT activities to add manually in Donkeyfolio. Solves the ServiceNow case (10.55 imported vs 21.56 in TR app — confirmed 2:1 split). 5-year lookback window, 300ms inter-call gap to respect Yahoo rate limits. (3) Cash reconciliation panel — pre-import, the addon now shows the PDF statement totals (opening / IN / OUT / closing), a per-activity-type breakdown of what we'll import (count + total + cash impact, +/- sign), and a verdict line: 'Statement Δ vs Activities Δ vs Gap'. When the gap is < €0.01 you're green. When it's not, the panel tells you what's likely wrong (parser dropped a row, or a TR Refund/Settlement/Rounding type isn't classified). v2.6.7: ticker map corrections after user verification (AVAV, DOCN, SOLST, MSTR, PNG.TO). v2.6.6: ISIN→ticker map (130+ entries) for live prices + logos. v2.6.5: defensive error coercion (Tauri IPC errors are strings). v2.6.4: critical fix — quoteCcy + instrumentType (was 0 imported / 3971 skipped). v2.6.3: account picker. v2.6.2: switch to single-create() per activity (bulk discards symbols).
